food_name: Coffee with Milk, Less Sugar (Bottled, Ready-to-Drink)
aliases: Coffee with Milk, Less Sugar (Bottled, Ready-to-Drink)

english: Coffee with Milk, Less Sugar (Bottled, Ready-to-Drink)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class (FOODON_03301737); no specific class exists for ready-to-drink bottled milk coffee with reduced sugar. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific sub-code for RTD bottled milk coffee with less sugar. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for bottled ready-to-drink milk coffee with reduced sugar. Null retained to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve bottle of ready-to-drink milk coffee with less sugar is approximately 240 ml (240 g), providing about 144 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee with milk, less sugar (bottled) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D09010021 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status 'reviewed'. All per_100g macro and micronutrient values are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for canned coffee with milk (USDA NDB 14209) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this adaptation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bottled less-sugar milk coffee product. GI estimated at 41 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for coffee with milk and sugar (GI ~41, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The reduced-sugar formulation (9g/100g sugar) is consistent with this GI range. Glycemic load per 240g serving = (41 × 13g carbohydrate per 100g × 2.4) / 100 = 12.8 × 0.41 ≈ 12.8; corrected: GL = GI/100 × carbohydrate_g_per_serving = 0.41 × (13 × 2.4) = 0.41 × 31.2 = 12.8. Note: GL per 100g = (41 × 13) / 100 = 5.33; per serving (240g): GL = (41 × 31.2) / 100 = 12.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 40mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Despite low sodium (40mg/100g), the 9g/100g sugar content classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage that independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should limit intake and prefer the no-sugar bottled version or plain black coffee.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: This bottled milk coffee contains 9g sugar per 100g with no dietary fibre, making it a sugar-sweetened beverage that causes rapid glucose absorption; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product and choose the no-sugar bottled version (≤1g sugar/100g) or unsweetened black coffee.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar at 9g/100g in a liquid matrix promotes dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation; fat content is low at 0.75g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The 9g/100g added sugar in this ready-to-drink coffee contributes to cardiovascular risk through sugar-driven dyslipidaemia and inflammation; fat content is low (0.75g/100g) and sodium is low (40mg/100g), but regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased CVD risk and should be minimised.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 9g/100g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, negating the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of coffee
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The added sugar in this bottled milk coffee promotes inflammatory mediators and negates the anti-inflammatory chlorogenic acid benefit of coffee; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose the no-sugar bottled version or plain black coffee.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coffee with Milk, Less Sugar (Bottled, Ready-to-Drink) is a commercially prepared, ultra-processed ready-to-drink (RTD) coffee beverage sold in bottles, widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is formulated with water, coffee extract, milk, and a reduced quantity of added sugar compared to standard sweetened milk coffee products. The 'less sugar' designation indicates that added sugar is present but at a lower level than the full-sugar equivalent; the HPB Singapore Foodo database records 9g sugar per 100g, which still classifies this product as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under the >5g/100g beverage threshold. Per 100g, the product provides 60 kcal, 1g protein, 0.75g fat, 13g carbohydrate, 9g sugar, 0g dietary fibre, and 40mg sodium. The beverage has a smooth, mildly sweet coffee-milk flavour with a light body and no bitterness. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or on-the-go beverage in Singapore, Malaysia, and the broader Southeast Asian region. Clinically, the high sugar content (9g/100g) and zero dietary fibre make this product unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, as liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly and independently raises blood pressure and cardiovascular risk. The low sodium content (40mg/100g) is a positive attribute but does not offset the sugar-related risks. Individuals seeking a healthier alternative should choose the no-sugar bottled version or plain black coffee.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database (cr_id D09010021, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (canned coffee with milk) and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry with clear documentation. All hard gates satisfied.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.5g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for canned/bottled coffee with milk (NDB 14209 or closest SR Legacy equivalent) as HPB Singapore Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this adaptation is documented per source policy. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific bottled RTD milk coffee product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. calcium_mg and iron_mg are set to 0.0 as confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo source record (per_100g_meta confidence 0.95). 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; this is expected for a liquid RTD coffee beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients. 5. GI of 41 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) for coffee with milk and sugar; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Glycemic load per 240g serving = (41 × 31.2) / 100 = 12.8. 6. The 'less sugar' label confirms added sugar is present; the product qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage under the >5g/100g threshold used in Singapore HPB and AHA 2018 guidelines. 7. Halal status assumed based on typical commercial bottled coffee products in Singapore; verification against specific brand certification is recommended. 8. dietary_tags 'low_sodium' (40mg/100g) and 'low_calorie' (60kcal/100g) from input cultural tags are factually supportable but do not offset the SSB sugar classification for clinical purposes.
